title: Hainanese Chicken Rice
description: My Thermomix version of Adam Liaw's Hainanese chicken rice — silky poached chicken and fragrant rice, cooked together.
keywords: adam liaw hainanese chicken rice thermomix
intro: Adam Liaw's Hainanese chicken rice is comfort food perfected. The Thermomix does the clever bit — the rice cooks in the bowl while the chicken steams above it in the Varoma, soaking up all the flavour.
ingredients: To serve: soy, sliced cucumber, chilli-ginger sauce
steps: Place the garlic and ginger in the bowl and chop 3 sec / 0°C / speed 7. Add the oil and sauté 3 min / 120°C / speed 1.
Add the rinsed rice and toast 2 min / 100°C / Reverse / speed 1, then add the stock.

Rub the chicken with a little ginger and the spring onions and place in the Varoma dish. Set the Varoma in position. Cook 25 min / Varoma / Reverse / speed 1 — the rice cooks below while the chicken steams above.
Rest the chicken 5 minutes, slice and serve over the rice with soy, cucumber and chilli-ginger sauce; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: Steaming keeps the chicken silky — that's the Hainanese way.
Check the chicken reaches 75°C in the thickest part.

faq.q: Can the Thermomix cook the chicken and rice together?
faq.a: Yes — the rice cooks in the bowl and the chicken steams in the Varoma above, in one 25-minute step.

faq.q: Is this the original Adam Liaw recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Adam Liaw and Hainanese chicken rice; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
